emotional_tone: High-energy, competitive, retro-futuristic, aggressive but playful

fonts.usage: H1, H2, Game Title, Major Call-to-Actions. Never use for long text.
Scores, Player Names, Health Bars, Bracket Nodes. Gives the 8-bit arcade feel.
Inputs, small labels, room codes, generic UI text.

scales.h1: font-['Unbounded'] text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-black tracking-tighter uppercase
scales.h2: font-['Unbounded'] text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight uppercase
scales.small_label: font-['JetBrains_Mono'] text-xs sm:text-sm text-[#8A95C7] tracking-[0.2em] uppercase

textures.crt_scanlines: A pointer-events-none overlay fixed to the screen with a repeating-linear-gradient to simulate CRT monitor lines.
textures.retro_grid: A subtle background grid pattern using linear-gradient to ground the UI.

layouts.global: Always wrap the main app in a container with bg-[#05050A] text-[#E0E5FF] min-h-screen overflow-x-hidden relative. Add the CRT scanline overlay.

the_forge.container: max-w-7xl mx-auto p-4 sm:p-8 grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-8
the_forge.canvas_panel: lg:col-span-6 flex flex-col items-center justify-center bg-[#0A0A14] border border-[#00F0FF]/30 p-8 relative

the_arena.container: relative w-full h-screen bg-[#000000] flex flex-col
the_arena.bracket_overlay: absolute inset-0 z-40 bg-[#05050A]/90 backdrop-blur-md flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-8

pixel_canvas.styling: width: 100%; max-width: 512px; aspect-ratio: 1/1; image-rendering: pixelated; border: 2px solid #4F588A; background: #0A0A14; cursor: crosshair;
pixel_canvas.interaction: Draw on a 64x64 internal resolution, scaled up via CSS. Include a grid overlay toggle.

buttons.primary: bg-[#00F0FF] text-[#05050A] font-['Unbounded'] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest px-8 py-4 rounded-none hover:bg-[#E0E5FF] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(0,240,255,0.8)] transition-all duration-200 border-b-4 border-[#007AFF] active:border-b-0 active:translate-y-[4px]
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-[#00F0FF] border border-[#00F0FF]/50 font-['JetBrains_Mono'] px-6 py-3 rounded-none hover:bg-[#00F0FF]/10 hover:border-[#00F0FF] transition-all
buttons.element_toggle: flex items-center justify-center w-full py-4 text-xl font-['Unbounded'] uppercase border-2 transition-all. If Fire: border-[#FF3366] text-[#FF3366] hover:bg-[#FF3366]/20. If Water: border-[#007AFF] text-[#007AFF] hover:bg-[#007AFF]/20.

inputs.text: bg-[#0A0A14] border-b-2 border-[#4F588A] text-[#E0E5FF] font-['JetBrains_Mono'] text-xl px-4 py-3 rounded-none focus:outline-none focus:border-[#00F0FF] focus:bg-[#141423] transition-colors placeholder:text-[#4F588A]

bracket_node.styling: bg-[#0A0A14] border border-[#4F588A] min-w-[160px] p-3 flex flex-col gap-2 relative z-10

bracket_lines.styling: Use absolute positioned SVG <path> elements to draw connections between nodes. Stroke color #4F588A for pending matches, #00F0FF for active path, #39FF14 for winner path.

images.notes: No external images required. The entire aesthetic relies on pure CSS, neon colors, typography, and HTML5 canvas drawings. The stickmen and weapons are drawn dynamically.

instructions_to_main_agent: Inject the Google Fonts (Unbounded, VT323, JetBrains Mono) into the main HTML file.

Implement the CRT Scanline effect as a global fixed div with pointer-events-none: `background: linear-gradient(rgba(18, 16, 16, 0) 50%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25) 50%), linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.06), rgba(0, 255, 0, 0.02), rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.06)); background-size: 100% 4px, 6px 100%;`

For the drawing canvas, ensure the logic uses a 64x64 array. The UI HTML <canvas> MUST have `image-rendering: pixelated;` or `image-rendering: crisp-edges;` so the pixels are sharp squares, not blurry.
For Shadcn components (if used for Dialogs/Modals), strip away the Vercel styling. Remove rounded corners (use `rounded-none`), apply dark backgrounds (`bg-[#0A0A14]`), and add sharp neon borders.
Build the Tournament Bracket using a flex/grid layout and use an SVG layer positioned absolutely behind the nodes to draw the connecting bracket lines.

For Phaser (The Arena), instantiate the game inside a specific `div` container. Ensure the Phaser canvas background is transparent so the CSS background bleeds through, OR make the Phaser background `#05050A`.
Add glowing effects to stickman attacks in Phaser using `setTint()` and blend modes like `Phaser.BlendModes.ADD`.
Always include `data-testid` on buttons, inputs, canvas, color pickers, and tournament bracket nodes for testing.
